devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । अथादीक्षत राजा तु हयमेधशतेन सः । ब्रह्मावर्ते मनोः क्षेत्रे यत्र प्राची सरस्वती ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca athādīkṣata rājā tu hayamedhaśatena saḥ brahmāvarte manoḥ kṣetre yatra prācī sarasvatī

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
The king (Pṛthu), thereupon, consecrated himself with the intention of performing one hundred horse-sacrifices at Brahmāvarta, the land of Manu, where the Sarasvatī flows towards the east.

devanagari: तदभिप्रेत्य भगवान् कर्मातिशयमात्मनः । शतक्रतुर्न ममृषे पृथोर्यज्ञमहोत्सवम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: tadabhipretya bhagavān karmātiśayamātmanaḥ śatakraturna mamṛṣe pṛthoryajñamahotsavam

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Anticipating that Pṛthu would excel his record performance of sacrifices, glorious Indra grew jealous of Pṛthu’s grand festival of sacrifices.

devanagari: यत्र यज्ञपतिः साक्षाद् भगवान् हरिरीश्वरः । अन्वभूयत सर्वात्मा सर्वलोकगुरुः प्रभुः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yajñapatiḥ sākṣād bhagavān harirīśvaraḥ anvabhūyata sarvātmā sarvalokaguruḥ prabhuḥ
translation: In that festival of sacrifices, the glorious Lord Hari; the Soul of all beings, the revered father of all the world, and the ruler of the universe, was perceived directly as the Lord of the sacrifices by all.

devanagari: अन्वितो ब्रह्मशर्वाभ्यां लोकपालैः सहानुगैः । उपगीयमानो गन्धर्वैः मुनिभिश्चाप्सरोगणैः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: anvito brahmaśarvābhyāṃ lokapālaiḥ sahānugaiḥ upagīyamāno gandharvaiḥ munibhiścāpsarogaṇaiḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: He was accompanied by Brahmā, Śiva and protectors of the world along with their followers. His glories were being sung by Gandharvas (celestial musicians), sages and troupes of Apsaras (heavenly nymphs).

devanagari: सिद्धा विद्याधरा दैत्या दानवा गुह्यकादयः । सुनन्दनन्दप्रमुखाः पार्षदप्रवरा हरेः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhā vidyādharā daityā dānavā guhyakādayaḥ sunandanandapramukhāḥ pārṣadapravarā hareḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sidh class 1 verb]

translation: Semidivine beings like Siddhas and Vidyādharas, Daityas, Dānavas, Guhyakas and others and Lord Hari’s prominent attendants headed by Sunanda and Nanda, Kapila, Nārada, Datta and the great masters of Yoga like Sanaka and others—all devotees of the Lord who were eager to serve the Lord, accompanied him.

devanagari: कपिलो नारदो दत्तो योगेशाः सनकादयः । तमन्वीयुर्भागवता ये च तत्सेवनोत्सुकाः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: kapilo nārado datto yogeśāḥ sanakādayaḥ tamanvīyurbhāgavatā ye ca tatsevanotsukāḥ
translation: Semidivine beings like Siddhas and Vidyādharas, Daityas, Dānavas, Guhyakas and others and Lord Hari’s prominent attendants headed by Sunanda and Nanda, Kapila, Nārada, Datta and the great masters of Yoga like Sanaka and others—all devotees of the Lord who were eager to serve the Lord, accompanied him.

devanagari: यत्र धर्मदुघा भूमिः सर्वकामदुघा सती । दोग्धि स्माभीप्सितान् अर्थान् यजमानस्य भारत ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra dharmadughā bhūmiḥ sarvakāmadughā satī dogdhi smābhīpsitān arthān yajamānasya bhārata
translation: Oh descendant of Bharata (Vidura)! In that sacrifice the earth which supplies the materials for oblations in the sacrifice acted as a wish-yielding heavenly cow, and gave as milk, all the objects desired and required by the sacrificer Pṛthu.

devanagari: ऊहुः सर्वरसान्नद्यः क्षीरदध्यन्नगोरसान् । तरवो भूरिवर्ष्माणः प्रासूयन्त मधुच्युतः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: ūhuḥ sarvarasānnadyaḥ kṣīradadhyannagorasān taravo bhūrivarṣmāṇaḥ prāsūyanta madhucyutaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ ūy class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ūy class 1 verb]

translation: The rivers flowed flooded with all kinds of sweet juices like those of sugarcane, grapes etc. as well as with milk, curds, food, ghee and butter-milk. Trees of gigantic dimensions which oozed out plentiful honey bore fruits abundantly.

devanagari: सिन्धवो रत्‍ननिकरान् गिरयोऽन्नं चतुर्विधम् । उपायनं उपाजह्रुः सर्वे लोकाः सपालकाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sindhavo rat‍nanikarān girayo upāyanaṃ upājahruḥ sarve lokāḥ sapālakāḥ
translation: The oceans brought to him heaps of precious stones as tributes; mountains, four kinds of food; all people along with their protectors[1] paid tributes to him,
purport:
Footnote [1]:
lokapālāḥ [lokapāla]—Protectors of the world like Indra—Bālaprabodhini

devanagari: इति चाधोक्षजेशस्य पृथोस्तु परमोदयम् । असूयन् भगवान् इन्द्रः प्रतिघातमचीकरत् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: iti cādhokṣajeśasya pṛthostu paramodayam asūyan bhagavān indraḥ pratighātamacīkarat

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ ūy class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ ūy class 1 verb]

translation: The mighty Indra grew jealous of this great prosperity of Pṛthu who regarded god Viṣṇu as his Master, and created obstacles in his way (to sabotage his scheme of hundred horse-sacrifices).

devanagari: चरमेणाश्वमेधेन यजमाने यजुष्पतिम् । वैन्ये यज्ञपशुं स्पर्धन् अपोवाह तिरोहितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: carameṇāśvamedhena yajamāne yajuṣpatim vainye yajñapaśuṃ spardhan apovāha tirohitaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [locative single from √ yaj class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yaj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ yaj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ spṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛdh class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While Pṛthu, the son of Vena, was propitiating Viṣṇu, the Lord of Sacrifices, with the last (hundredth) horsesacrifice, the jealous Indra carried away the sacrificial horse, himself remaining invisible.

devanagari: तं अत्रिर्भगवानैक्षत् त्वरमाणं विहायसा । आमुक्तमिव पाखण्डं योऽधर्मे धर्मविभ्रमः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ atrirbhagavānaikṣat tvaramāṇaṃ vihāyasā āmuktamiva pākhaṇḍaṃ yo

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: The venerable sage Atri noticed him hurrying through the sky in the disguise of a heretic who mistakes unrighteousness for righteousness and the guise served him like an armour.

devanagari: अत्रिणा चोदितो हन्तुं पृथुपुत्रो महारथः । अन्वधावत सङ्क्रुद्धः तिष्ठ तिष्ठेति चाब्रवीत् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: atriṇā codito hantuṃ pṛthuputro mahārathaḥ anvadhāvata saṅkruddhaḥ tiṣṭha tiṣṭheti cābravīt

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: As directed by Atri, the son of Pṛthu, who was a great warrior, pursued Indra to kill him, and shouted to him in rage “Halt! Halt”.

devanagari: तं तादृशाकृतिं वीक्ष्य मेने धर्मं शरीरिणम् । जटिलं भस्मनाच्छन्नं तस्मै बाणं न मुञ्चति ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tādṛśākṛtiṃ vīkṣya mene dharmaṃ śarīriṇam jaṭilaṃ bhasmanācchannaṃ tasmai bāṇaṃ na muñcati

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Seeing Indra in that sage-like form with matted hair and body besmeared with sacred ashes, the son of Pṛthu thought him to be Dharma (piousness, religion) incarnate, and did not (feel disposed to) shoot an arrow at him.

devanagari: वधान्निवृत्तं तं भूयो हन्तवेऽत्रिरचोदयत् । जहि यज्ञहनं तात महेन्द्रं विबुधाधमम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: vadhānnivṛttaṃ taṃ bhūyo hantave jahi yajñahanaṃ tāta mahendraṃ vibudhādhamam
translation: When the Prince returned without killing Indra, Atri urged him again to slay him, saying, “Oh child, kill this great Indra, who is the meanest of gods as he had obstructed your father’s sacrifice”.

devanagari: एवं वैन्यसुतः प्रोक्तः त्वरमाणं विहायसा । अन्वद्रवद् अभिक्रुद्धो रावणं गृध्रराडिव ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ vainyasutaḥ proktaḥ tvaramāṇaṃ vihāyasā anvadravad abhikruddho rāvaṇaṃ gṛdhrarāḍiva

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: Thus incited (by Atri), the son of Pṛthu, got furious and chased Indra who was fleeing through the sky as Jaṭāyu[2] (the king of vultures) pursued Rāvaṇa.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Jaṭāyu—A son of Aruṇa and Gṛdhrī; king of vultures and younger brother of Sampāti. He tried to prevent the abduction of Sītā by Rāvaṇa, and was killed by Rāvaṇa’s treachery. His funeral was performed by Rāma.

devanagari: सोऽश्वं रूपं च तद्धित्वा तस्मा अन्तर्हितः स्वराट् । वीरः स्वपशुमादाय पितुर्यज्ञं उपेयिवान् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: so vīraḥ svapaśumādāya pituryajñaṃ upeyivān
translation: The King of the Heaven (Indra) gave up his own form and the sacrificial horse and disappeared. Taking with him his own sacrificial horse, the hero (Pṛthu’s son) returned to (the place of) his father’s sacrifice.

devanagari: तत्तस्य चाद्‍भुतं कर्म विचक्ष्य परमर्षयः । नामधेयं ददुस्तस्मै विजिताश्व इति प्रभो ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tattasya cād‍bhutaṃ karma vicakṣya paramarṣayaḥ nāmadheyaṃ dadustasmai vijitāśva iti prabho
translation: Witnessing his miraculous deed, the great sages conferred on him the (significant) epithet Vijitāśva (conqueror of the horse), Oh Lord (Vidura)!

devanagari: उपसृज्य तमस्तीव्रं जहाराश्वं पुनर्हरिः । चषालयूपतश्छन्नो हिरण्यरशनं विभुः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: upasṛjya tamastīvraṃ jahārāśvaṃ punarhariḥ caṣālayūpataśchanno hiraṇyaraśanaṃ vibhuḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The mighty Indra created very thick darkness under the cover of which he stole again the horse tied with a gold chain from the sacrificial post with the circular wooden ring called caṣāla on its top.

devanagari: अत्रिः सन्दर्शयामास त्वरमाणं विहायसा । कपालखट्वाङ्गधरं वीरो नैनमबाधत ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: atriḥ sandarśayāmāsa tvaramāṇaṃ vihāyasā kapālakhaṭvāṅgadharaṃ vīro nainamabādhata

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: Atri (again) pointed out Indra hurrying through the sky. But the warrior (Pṛthu’s son) did not molest him as he was carrying a human skull and Khaṭvāṅga (a club with a skull at the top) in his hands (like a Kāpālaka).

devanagari: अत्रिणा चोदितस्तस्मै सन्दधे विशिखं रुषा । सोऽश्वं रूपं च तद्धित्वा तस्थावन्तर्हितः स्वराट् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: atriṇā coditastasmai sandadhe viśikhaṃ ruṣā so

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[present middle first single], [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Being urged by Atri, however, he indignantly fixed an arrow (on the bow) aiming it at Indra. The king of heaven (Indra) gave up the sacrificial horse, and his guise (as Kāpālika) and stayed invisible.

devanagari: वीरश्चाश्वमुपादाय पितृयज्ञमथाव्रजत् । तदवद्यं हरे रूपं जगृहुर्ज्ञानदुर्बलाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: vīraścāśvamupādāya pitṛyajñamathāvrajat tadavadyaṃ hare rūpaṃ jagṛhurjñānadurbalāḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: Taking the horse with him, the hero (Pṛthu’s son) returned to (the place of) his father’s sacrifice, while people of poor understanding accepted the censurable guise assumed (and left) by Indra.

devanagari: यानि रूपाणि जगृहे इन्द्रो हयजिहीर्षया । तानि पापस्य खण्डानि लिङ्गं खण्डमिहोच्यते ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yāni rūpāṇi jagṛhe indro hayajihīrṣayā tāni pāpasya khaṇḍāni liṅgaṃ khaṇḍamihocyate
translation: Whatever forms were assumed by Indra with the intention of carrying away the consecrated horse, were the marks of sins. Here the word khaṇḍa is used in the sense of “mark”.

devanagari: एवमिन्द्रे हरत्यश्वं वैन्ययज्ञजिघांसया । तद्‍गृहीतविसृष्टेषु पाखण्डेषु मतिर्नृणाम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: evamindre haratyaśvaṃ vainyayajñajighāṃsayā tad‍gṛhītavisṛṣṭeṣu pākhaṇḍeṣu matirnṛṇām

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [locative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]

translation: In this way while carrying away the horse with the intention of interrupting Pṛthu’s sacrifice, Indra assumed and abandoned deceitful disguise of nude ascetics (Digambara Jainas), red-robed ascetics (Buddhists) and others (such as Kāpālakas). Minds of men, through their misapprehension (of the true teachings of the Vedas), are generally attracted to these pseudo-religious sects as the true religion, as they are charmingly presented with excellent eloquence.

devanagari: धर्म इत्युपधर्मेषु नग्नरक्तपटादिषु । प्रायेण सज्जते भ्रान्त्या पेशलेषु च वाग्मिषु ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharma ityupadharmeṣu nagnaraktapaṭādiṣu prāyeṇa sajjate bhrāntyā peśaleṣu ca vāgmiṣu
translation: In this way while carrying away the horse with the intention of interrupting Pṛthu’s sacrifice, Indra assumed and abandoned deceitful disguise of nude ascetics (Digambara Jainas), red-robed ascetics (Buddhists) and others (such as Kāpālakas). Minds of men, through their misapprehension (of the true teachings of the Vedas), are generally attracted to these pseudo-religious sects as the true religion, as they are charmingly presented with excellent eloquence.

devanagari: तदभिज्ञाय भगवान् पृथुः पृथुपराक्रमः । इन्द्राय कुपितो बाणं आदत्तोद्यतकार्मुकः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tadabhijñāya bhagavān pṛthuḥ pṛthuparākramaḥ indrāya kupito bāṇaṃ ādattodyatakārmukaḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ kup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ kup class 10 verb]

translation: When the illustrious king Pṛthu of great prowess learnt this, he got infuriated. He took up his bow and aimed an arrow at Indra.

devanagari: तमृत्विजः शक्रवधाभिसन्धितं । विचक्ष्य दुष्प्रेक्ष्यमसह्यरंहसम् । निवारयामासुरहो महामते । न युज्यतेऽत्रान्यवधः प्रचोदितात् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tamṛtvijaḥ śakravadhābhisandhitaṃ vicakṣya duṣprekṣyamasahyaraṃhasam nivārayāmāsuraho mahāmate na yujyate
translation: Finding that Pṛthu was determined to kill Indra and appeared of irresistible vehemence and terrible to look at, the sacrificial priests prevented him urging, “Oh highly intelligent and wise king, it is not proper to kill anything else here (at the sacrifice) except the consecrated beast (as enjoined by Vedas).

devanagari: वयं मरुत्वन्तमिहार्थनाशनं । ह्वयामहे त्वच्छ्रवसा हतत्विषम् । अयातयामोपहवैरनन्तरं । प्रसह्य राजन् जुहवाम तेऽहितम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: vayaṃ marutvantamihārthanāśanaṃ hvayāmahe tvacchravasā hatatviṣam ayātayāmopahavairanantaraṃ prasahya rājan juhavāma te

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: By the force of Vedic incantation of undiminished power, we shall now invoke (and forcibly bring) Indra, the Lord of Maruts who has interrupted your object (of performing this sacrifice) and has lost his glory by your high reputation. Oh King, we shall then forcibly offer your enemy as an oblation to fire.”

devanagari: इत्यामन्त्र्य क्रतुपतिं विदुरास्यर्त्विजो रुषा । स्रुग्घस्तान् जुह्वतोऽभ्येत्य स्वयम्भूः प्रत्यषेधत ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: ityāmantrya kratupatiṃ vidurāsyartvijo ruṣā srugghastān juhvato

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [vocative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [accusative plural from √ hu class 3 verb], [genitive single from √ hu class 3 verb]
[ablative single from √ hu class 3 verb], [genitive single from √ hu class 3 verb]

translation: Oh Vidura! Having thus addressed Pṛthu, the master of the sacrifice, his sacrificial priests, with the ladle (Śruc) in their hands, indignantly proceeded to pour oblations in the sacrificial fire, when the self-born god Brahmā appeared and prevented them (from doing so saying).

devanagari: न वध्यो भवतां इन्द्रो यद्यज्ञो भगवत्तनुः । यं जिघांसथ यज्ञेन यस्येष्टास्तनवः सुराः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: na vadhyo bhavatāṃ indro yadyajño bhagavattanuḥ yaṃ jighāṃsatha yajñena yasyeṣṭāstanavaḥ surāḥ
translation: “Indra whom you wish to kill by means of the sacrifice, should not be killed. For Indra designated as Yajña is the manifestation of the Almighty Lord (himself), and gods whom you offered oblations are (mere) forms of Indra.”

devanagari: तदिदं पश्यत महद् धर्मव्यतिकरं द्विजाः । इन्द्रेणानुष्ठितं राज्ञः कर्मैतद्विजिघांसता ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadidaṃ paśyata mahad dharmavyatikaraṃ dvijāḥ indreṇānuṣṭhitaṃ rājñaḥ karmaitadvijighāṃsatā
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇas! consider this great violation of Dharma (leading to propagation of heretic sects) perpetrated by Indra who desires to interrupt the king’s performance of the sacrifice. (He may again encourage heretic doctrines to the detriment of the world.)

devanagari: पृथुकीर्तेः पृथोर्भूयात् तर्ह्येकोनशतक्रतुः । अलं ते क्रतुभिः स्विष्टैः यद्‍भवान् मोक्षधर्मवित् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛthukīrteḥ pṛthorbhūyāt tarhyekonaśatakratuḥ alaṃ te kratubhiḥ sviṣṭaiḥ yad‍bhavān mokṣadharmavit
translation: Therefore may Pṛthu who has performed ninety-nine sacrifices be more famous than Indra” (Brahmā then addressed to Pṛthu): “As Your honour knows the path leading to Liberation, the sacrifices which you have so magnificently performed are enough.

devanagari: नैवात्मने महेन्द्राय रोषमाहर्तुमर्हसि । उभावपि हि भद्रं ते उत्तमश्लोकविग्रहौ ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: naivātmane mahendrāya roṣamāhartumarhasi ubhāvapi hi bhadraṃ te uttamaślokavigrahau
translation: May you be blessed (despite the non-completion of this sacrifice). Both you and Indra are the embodiments of Viṣṇu of excellent renown. Hence, it is not proper to cherish anger against the great Indra who is your own self.

devanagari: मास्मिन्महाराज कृथाः स्म चिन्तां । निशामयास्मद्वच आदृतात्मा । यद्ध्यायतो दैवहतं नु कर्तुं । मनोऽतिरुष्टं विशते तमोऽन्धम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: māsminmahārāja kṛthāḥ sma cintāṃ niśāmayāsmadvaca ādṛtātmā yaddhyāyato daivahataṃ nu kartuṃ mano

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[accusative single from √ ruṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ruṣ class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ ruṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ruṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ruṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ ruṣ class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ ruś class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ ruś class 6 verb]

translation: Oh great king! Please do not worry about this (interruption or non-completion of the sacrifice). Listen to our words with respectful attention. The mind of the person who contemplates to accomplish what has been frustrated by the Fate, certainly gets extremely infuriated and enters blinding darkness of delusion[3] (and does not get peace).
purport:
Footnote [3]:
v.l. mano'ti-kaṣṭam: The mind falls into the darkest hell. This suggests both the reason and undesirable consequences of such attempts against the gods.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: क्रतुर्विरमतामेष देवेषु दुरवग्रहः । धर्मव्यतिकरो यत्र पाखण्डैः इन्द्रनिर्मितैः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: kraturviramatāmeṣa deveṣu duravagrahaḥ dharmavyatikaro yatra pākhaṇḍaiḥ indranirmitaiḥ
translation: Let this sacrifice be stopped now for there is obstinacy in gods.[4] It is here (due to this sacrifice) that Dharma was violated by heretics created by Indra.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
You should give up your prejudice against gods, for the gods, if enraged, will obstruct rainfall and famine will devastate the land.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: एभिरिन्द्रोपसंसृष्टैः पाखण्डैर्हारिभिर्जनम् । ह्रियमाणं विचक्ष्वैनं यस्ते यज्ञध्रुगश्वमुट् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: ebhirindropasaṃsṛṣṭaiḥ pākhaṇḍairhāribhirjanam hriyamāṇaṃ vicakṣvainaṃ yaste yajñadhrugaśvamuṭ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ hṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [locative single from √ yas class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ yas class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]

translation: Have a look at these people who are being enticed away by attractive heretical doctrines propagated by Indra who maliciously plotted against your sacrifice and stole away the sacrificial horse.

devanagari: भवान्परित्रातुमिहावतीर्णो । धर्मं जनानां समयानुरूपम् । वेनापचारादवलुप्तमद्य । तद्देहतो विष्णुकलासि वैन्य ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavānparitrātumihāvatīrṇo dharmaṃ janānāṃ samayānurūpam venāpacārādavaluptamadya taddehato viṣṇukalāsi vainya

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [accusative single from √ lup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ lup class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ lup class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ lup class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ lup class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ lup class 6 verb]

translation: Oh son of Vena! You are a part of Viṣṇu incarnated on this earth now from Vena’s body to protect the religion[5] of the people suitable to the present times—religion that declined by the misdeeds of Vena.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Religious tenets based on Vedas and orthodox philosophic systems like Sāṃkhya, Yoga etc.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: स त्वं विमृश्यास्य भवं प्रजापते । सङ्कल्पनं विश्वसृजां पिपीपृहि । ऐन्द्रीं च मायामुपधर्ममातरं । प्रचण्डपाखण्डपथं प्रभो जहि ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tvaṃ vimṛśyāsya bhavaṃ prajāpate saṅkalpanaṃ viśvasṛjāṃ pipīpṛhi aindrīṃ ca māyāmupadharmamātaraṃ pracaṇḍapākhaṇḍapathaṃ prabho jahi
translation: [6] Oh sovereign ruler of the people! Such a ray of Viṣṇu as you are, you consider the origination of the universe[7] and fulfil the purpose of the proginators of the world (like Bhṛgu and others who created you.) Oh Lord! destroy the formidable heretic path—the illusion created by Indra which is the source of pseudo-religious sects”.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī differs: As you have incarnated yourself for the protection of religion, you should consider that your performance of the intended hundredth sacrifice will be an insult to Indra (Prajāpati) and as such you should not perform it. You should completely fulfil the desire of Indra that you should not complete the hundredth horse-sacrifice. Taking into account the all-round auspicious effects of not opposing Indra’s will, you obey the desire of the creator of the universe (viz. myself) that you should perform only ninety-nine sacrifices. By this sacrifice or religious conduct you should destroy the formidable heretic path which is the illusion created by Indra—a source of pseudo-religious creeds.

Footnote [7]:
VC. and Siddhāntapradīpa interpret bhava as the ‘good’ of the universe.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इत्थं स लोकगुरुणा समादिष्टो विशाम्पतिः । तथा च कृत्वा वात्सल्यं मघोनापि च सन्दधे ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca itthaṃ sa lokaguruṇā samādiṣṭo viśāmpatiḥ tathā ca kṛtvā vātsalyaṃ maghonāpi ca sandadhe

translation:
Maitreya said:
Being thus advised by Brahmā, the creator of the world gave up his iṇsistance on the performance of the hundredth sacrifice, adopted a friendly policy to Indra, and made peace with him.

devanagari: कृतावभृथस्नानाय पृथवे भूरिकर्मणे । वरान्ददुस्ते वरदा ये तद्‍बर्हिषि तर्पिताः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtāvabhṛthasnānāya pṛthave bhūrikarmaṇe varāndaduste varadā ye tad‍barhiṣi tarpitāḥ

translation: Those gods who were propitiated in the sacrifice and were disposed to confer blessings bestowed boons upon Pṛthu who had performed many auspicious deeds including the concluding ablution (avabhṛtha) at the end of the sacrificial session.

devanagari: विप्राः सत्याशिषस्तुष्टाः श्रद्धया लब्धदक्षिणाः । आशिषो युयुजुः क्षत्तः आदिराजाय सत्कृताः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: viprāḥ satyāśiṣastuṣṭāḥ śraddhayā labdhadakṣiṇāḥ āśiṣo yuyujuḥ kṣattaḥ ādirājāya satkṛtāḥ

[nominative plural from √ tuś class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tuś class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tuś class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Vidura! The Brāhmaṇas whose blessings were efficacious, and who were faithfully paid the sacrificial money and honoured, became satisfied and pronounced their blessings on the first monarch (Pṛthu).

devanagari: त्वयाऽहूता महाबाहो सर्व एव समागताः । पूजिता दानमानाभ्यां पितृदेवर्षिमानवाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: tvayā pūjitā dānamānābhyāṃ pitṛdevarṣimānavāḥ

analyses.morph_synt_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

[nominative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation:
Oh mighty-armed king! All the manes (Pitṛs), gods and sages who were invited by you, have attended and have been worshipped by you paying proper respects and gifts.”
